key_insight: SciPhyRL turns dynamic portfolio allocation into a data-driven physics-informed control problem by solving a pathwise Hamilton-Jacobi equation directly from offline trajectories.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical way to learn cost-aware, distribution-sensitive control policies from historical data without online trial-and-error, which is useful for large-scale decision systems under constraints.
method: Formulate portfolio optimization in continuous time with an extended state that includes cumulative costs.
Project the HJB equation onto observed paths and solve it with a PINN in a single offline pass.
Use a discrete target-holding control and a quadratic price-impact model to capture execution costs.
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on a 14-asset ETF universe with an engineered oracle signal and showed strong out-of-sample Sharpe ratio gains over static and myopic baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the engineered signal and the realism of the assumed price-impact model in live market conditions.
paper_title: SciPhy Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization
